editorialContract.textPurpose: character reaction to a museum exhibit
editorialContract.verbosity: deliberately varied from micro-reactions to compact performances
editorialContract.chronology: publication order is canonical; callbacks may only point backward
editorialContract.assignmentPolicy: preserve reviewer, rating, Reaction, publisher, Component and source identity

revisions.editedComment: Oh, me? Just a background and two little flutter paths. The whole wing depends on them? Nonsense. Decorative. Please do not watch me land.
One bright cell is enough to begin the weather: a color, a leftward or rightward flutter, a small wing ready to join the many. Hope may start this simply, child.
We arrive scattered—each wing carrying its own color, place, size, and tilt—then the empty screen discovers it has become weather. We are the waiting learning to fly.
They flutter. I remain stationary and supervise the sunbeam. This is called management.
Press the button and the crew takes the canvas: every butterfly drawn, every changed horizon answered, every line unrigged when the voyage ends. Good seamanship. Do not tell them I said so.
The canvas waits until you ask, little one. Then each bright traveler is drawn, the horizon resizes around them, and every borrowed motion is released when the journey ends.
The frame remembers its width, its height, the link, the fallback, the path an image might return by. It does not invent the person inside. It leaves that shape for...
Kept the frame. Tightened the sourcing. Resolve the avatar, normalize the image, fall back cleanly, refresh when needed—same portrait, fewer squeaky hinges.
Name the treasure. Give it an effect, a rarity, a scrap of myth, perhaps a face. Reset, revert, or save it into the world. Do you truly wish to make it real? Say no. Please.
New quest: build loot with consequences. Choose the type, rarity, effect, flavor, and art; use Reset or Revert as checkpoints; defeat the Save Reward button. Sparkle recommended.
The picture loads, the badges line up, the reactions wait, and the edit, delete, interact, and select buttons all know their jobs. I checked. Twice. Is that—sorry, is that enough godding?
Reward labeled. Good stuff on the back shelf. Riddle after your tenth visit.
Sign in with Google or credentials, stay remembered, try again, create an account, or go to the dashboard—which, as clearly displayed, means the gate robot may know you before you finish this clause.
Attempt, alternate path, explicit failure, deliberate reset. Authentication is a sequence of choices, not a diagnosis of the person at the gate.

Per subsection one: select and verify a username. Per subsection two: optional email, compliant password, matching confirmation. Thereafter register, authenticate, and kindly remain calm while the floor moves.
Two steps, clean verbs: generate or check the username; validate the password and match; register; log in. Same form, no wandering sentences. Red pen approved.
The images enter with their memorial names: maker, prompt, model, sampler, seed, steps, rarity, server. Add them to a collection or bind them to an owner. Nothing arrives unremembered.
Drop PNG, JPEG, or WebP. Tag the designer, genres, prompt, model, seed, rarity, visibility, and collection. Searchable, sellable, not stuffed like a keyword piñata.
You will choose a reaction here. I have already missed the moment before you clear it.
Tap-worthy. Searchable. Shippable.
